paper_title: Model Assessment and Development of eCA on Surface Boundary-Layer Chemistry

methods.method_name: Model Assessment of eCA on Surface Boundary-Layer Chemistry
methods.description: This method uses a spatially resolved reaction-diffusion model to quantify the effect of external carbonic anhydrase (eCA) on boundary-layer chemistry, particularly focusing on maintaining CO2 concentrations at the cell surface during photosynthesis.

steps.description: A model simulation was run in which a diatom takes up CO2 at two-thirds its photosynthetic rate, which is typical of cultured marine diatoms.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate diatom CO2 uptake at two-thirds photosynthetic rate

steps.description: Average photosynthetic rates measured in this study were used in the simulation.
steps.searchable_summary: use measured photosynthetic rates in simulation

steps.description: The analysis shows that the measured eCA activities are sufficient to maintain cell surface CO2 near bulk concentrations despite photosynthetic carbon uptake in both species.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze eCA activity effect on CO2 concentration

steps.description: Simulate the scenario where eCA could be involved in the control of surface pH.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate eCA effect on surface pH control

methods.description: This method involves developing a box model to consider 18O-CO2 and 18O-HCO3- isotopologs in three compartments: the bulk solution, the boundary layer at the cell surface, and the intracellular space.

steps.description: The model is described by a system of differential equations with variables and parameters as described in Table III.
steps.searchable_summary: develop differential equations for eCA model

steps.description: The solution volume (Ve) was measured directly, and the intracellular volume was determined using a Coulter Counter.
The uncatalyzed CO2 hydration/HCO3- dehydration rates in the bulk solution (kuf, kur) were determined from 18O removal rates prior to the addition of cells.
steps.searchable_summary: determine uncatalyzed reaction rates from 18O removal

steps.description: Calculate mass transfer coefficients for diffusive flux between the bulk solution and the boundary layer.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mass transfer coefficients for diffusive flux

steps.description: Determine eCA-catalyzed hydration/dehydration rate constants by optimizing the model fit to the 18O-CO2 data.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize model fit to determine eCA-catalyzed rate constants
